title: Bus message envelope
description: The on-disk format of one bus message. Owned by tools/bus.py; this schema is the canonical definition of the envelope.

id.description: Unique message id. A reply references it via in_reply_to; there is no separate request id.

to.description: Recipient: an agent id (directed), or `*` (everyone / broadcast).

in_reply_to.description: The id of the request this message answers. Present only on a reply.

notify_user.description: Present and true only when the sending agent intends the user to see this.

operator_origin.description: Present and true only when the message originates from the operator.

body.description: The payload. Optional; any type. A standard request's body is a fixed identifier string (e.g. "identity", "status") that the recipient's sidecar answers itself.

question_id.description: Present only on a bus.py `ask` broadcast (sidebar-polish item 12). A reply references it via the existing in_reply_to field — answers add no new envelope field of their own.

question.description: The question text, present only alongside question_id.

options.description: Numbered answer choices, present only alongside question_id; array index = display/option number.

title.description: Optional short title for a `bus.py ask` broadcast (sidebar-polish item 12g), shown prominently above the question in the popup. Present only alongside question_id.

summary.description: Optional short summary of what the decision is about, for a `bus.py ask` broadcast (item 12g). Present only alongside question_id.

multi.description: Present and true only when a `bus.py ask --multi` broadcast (item 12g): the popup is a multi-select (digits toggle, Enter confirms) rather than the default instant-on-digit single-select.
